mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) drives aggregate wage income and consumer spending, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns follow month-over-month PAYEMS increases in fewer than 55% of out-of-sample observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the restrictive cost of capital outweighs any residual growth premium, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as leveraged corporate cash flows are discounted more heavily.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of months when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the triggered signal's hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous level of consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) captures the real policy stance; when both are elevated, unexpectedly high real rates erode equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days that neither proxy generates independently.
failure_condition: The joint interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS produces a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms the linear sum of individual proxies.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month contractions in industrial production (INDPRO) signal impending capacity under-utilization and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this link vanishes in early recoveries when slack is abundant.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns follow INDPRO contractions in fewer than 55% of episodes when UNRATE is below 5%, or the signal is indistinguishable from zero in a two-sample t-test (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) capture high-frequency consumer demand surprises that immediately lift cyclical equity exposures, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns follow RSAFS increases in fewer than 52% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over a rolling 3-month window triggers recession probability repricing, reducing equity risk appetite and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or the average forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts (HOUST) transmit backward-linkage demand through construction and durable goods channels, raising cyclical earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of months when HOUST is above its trailing 12-month median.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month deceleration in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) reduces near-term inflation risk premium embedded in equity discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as terminal rate expectations shift down.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL deceleration is followed by positive 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of observations, or the mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).
